# feat(interp): port InterpretabilityDiagnostics to kaizen.interpretability

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `4cdc3b558f78` — feat(interp): port InterpretabilityDiagnostics to kaizen.interpretability

**Body**:

PR#4 of 7 in issue #567 (cross-SDK Diagnostic Protocol adoption). Ports
MLFP's interpretability lens to kailash-kaizen with production-neutral
naming (medical-metaphor sweep removed), Protocol conformance against
kailash.diagnostics.protocols.Diagnostic, and open-weight-only safety.

Implementation:

- new kaizen.interpretability package (greenfield namespace)
- InterpretabilityDiagnostics adapter — attention heatmaps (Llama/Gemma/
  Phi/Mistral), logit lens across layers, scikit-learn linear probes,
  optional Gemma-Scope SAE features
- lazy HF load via AutoModelForCausalLM + AutoTokenizer with
  local_files_only=True default; operators pass allow_download=True
  explicitly to opt into multi-GB network fetches
- deque(maxlen=window) on every per-analysis buffer per analysis §1.3
  VRAM guidance
- API-only model prefixes (gpt-/o1-/claude-/gemini-/deepseek-) refuse
  to load and return {"mode": "not_applicable"} — honest failure per
  zero-tolerance.md Rule 2
- _require_transformers / _require_sae_lens / _require_plotly /
  _require_matplotlib / _require_sklearn helpers raise ImportError
  naming pip install kailash-kaizen[interpretability] (observability.md
  loud-fail contract)
- structured INFO logs with interp_* domain prefix (observability.md
  MUST Rule 9 LogRecord-attribute-collision contract)

Not yet in this commit: tests, spec, pyproject extra, CHANGELOG
entry — follow in subsequent WIP commits per agents.md worktree
commit-discipline rule.

Refs #567 (PR#4 of 7)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
